meta.title: CJNG Cartel Retaliatory Violence — Nationwide, Feb 22–25, 2026
meta.subtitle: 85+ narcobloqueos and cartel attacks across 20+ states following the killing of "El Mencho" in Tapalpa, Jalisco

infoBox.vehicleTypes: Trucks, buses, taxis, cars, Oxxo stores, banks, government buildings
infoBox.status: Phase 1 (Feb 22-23): 90% cleared by Sunday night. Phase 2 (Feb 24+): Continued in Jalisco.
infoBox.coordNote: 74+ dead (27 security forces, 46+ CJNG/criminals, civilians). 70+ arrested. 10,000 soldiers deployed.

incidents.title: Military Operation Kills El Mencho
incidents.detail: Federal forces kill El Mencho (Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes) in Tapalpa during morning operation. 6 CJNG killed at scene, El Mencho died in airlift to hospital. CIA/JITC-CC intelligence support. Total 8 CJNG dead.

incidents.title: Guadalajara Metro Becomes Ghost Town
incidents.detail: Guadalajara metro paralyzed with 20+ narcobloqueos on Periférico, Avenida México, and Calle Federalismo. Gas stations on fire, multiple bank branches attacked. Public transit suspended (Mi Macro Bus, Tren Eléctrico). CJNG distributed threat videos and circulars throughout city demanding government negotiations.

incidents.title: Zapopan Multi-Site Fires and Confrontations
incidents.detail: Zapopan experienced coordinated attacks with 8 Oxxo stores burned on Avenida Mariano Otero and surrounding streets, 2 commercial banks torched. 12+ vehicles blockaded on main thoroughfares. 3 police vehicles damaged, 2 officers wounded. Heavy armed presence of gunmen reported near Plaza Patria.

incidents.title: Puerto Vallarta Crisis - Multiple Fires and Lockdown
incidents.detail: Puerto Vallarta locked down with 15+ narcobloqueos on Avenida Francisco Medina Ascencio and Highway 200. Multiple hotel zones affected, tourism paralyzed. 6 buses burned on roadways. Police presence minimal. CJNG maintained blockades for 36+ hours with rotating armed personnel.

incidents.title: Ciudad Guzmán Partial Blockades
incidents.detail: Ciudad Guzmán saw partial blockades on Avenida Jacarandas and bypass routes. 3 Oxxo stores looted and burned. 1 bank ATM destroyed with explosives. 8 vehicles abandoned. Highways to Colima partially affected. National Guard deployed by afternoon.

incidents.title: Chapala Highway Blockade
incidents.detail: Narcobloqueos and shootings on highway from Guadalajara to Chapala.

incidents.title: Tecolotlán Road Blockades
incidents.detail: Road blockades and vehicle burnings throughout Tecolotlán.

incidents.title: Mascota Highway Blockade
incidents.detail: Road blockade on highway toward Puerto Vallarta.

incidents.title: Tlajomulco Commercial Attacks
incidents.detail: Tlajomulco: 10+ commercial establishments attacked on Avenida Las Fuentes. 5 Oxxo stores burned, 2 pharmacies looted. 1 mini-supermarket destroyed. Cash registers emptied before fires set. 4 vehicles burned on nearby streets.

incidents.title: Car Bomb Attack in San Juan de los Lagos
incidents.detail: Car bomb detonated in San Juan de los Lagos town center on Avenida Benito Juárez, destroying 2 nearby vehicles and damaging storefront. 1 civilian killed, 4 wounded. Blast radius affected 50-meter area. Attack claimed to target police informants.

incidents.title: Penal de Ixtapa Prison Break
incidents.detail: Prison break incident at Penal de Ixtapa near Puerto Vallarta.

incidents.title: Guadalajara International Airport Disruption
incidents.detail: 49 flights cancelled (25%), 42 delayed (22%). American, Delta, United, Southwest, Alaska, WestJet, Air Canada suspensions. Panic in terminals. Reports of external gunfire.

incidents.title: León Mass Arson Campaign
incidents.detail: León city: 20+ stores attacked on Avenida Universidad and Paseo de la República. 8 Oxxo stores burned, 4 gas stations vandalized. 2 vehicles exploded near El Bajío neighborhood. Coordinated teams operating from 22:00 to 04:00 hours.

incidents.title: Guanajuato City Arson Attacks
incidents.detail: Incendiary attacks on commercial establishments in Guanajuato capital.

incidents.title: Irapuato Business Fires
incidents.detail: Incendiary attacks on businesses throughout Irapuato.

incidents.title: Silao Incendiary Attacks
incidents.detail: Incendiary attacks on commercial targets in Silao.

incidents.title: San Francisco del Rincón Oxxo Firebombing
incidents.detail: Oxxo firebombed with Molotov cocktail on Bulevar Las Torres.

incidents.title: Pénjamo Armed Confrontations
incidents.detail: Armed confrontations between gunmen and security forces in Pénjamo.

incidents.title: Cointzio Road Blockade
incidents.detail: Vehicles burned to block roads near Morelia. Widely photographed incidents.

incidents.title: Zitácuaro Road Blockades
incidents.detail: Blockade involved 33 municipalities across Michoacán experiencing simultaneous narco-roadblocks. Coordinated CJNG action spanning 6,000+ sq km territory. Federal highway 120 and state routes 33 municipalities affected. Military aircraft deployed for surveillance.

incidents.title: Numarán Highway Trailer Burning
incidents.detail: Trailer burned on Zináparo-La Piedad highway.

incidents.title: Jiquilpan Municipal Building Attack
incidents.detail: Vehicle burned, gunmen shot at municipal building.

incidents.title: Churintzio Vehicle Burning
incidents.detail: Truck driver forced out, vehicle burned.

incidents.title: Zamora Region Blockades
incidents.detail: Zamora-La Piedad carretera (Federal 110) paralyzed with 12 burning trucks and buses. 6 toll booths vandalized. 2 highway patrol vehicles attacked, 1 officer killed. Road remained impassable for 40 hours. State forces eventually cleared debris.

incidents.title: Buenavista-Tepalcatepec Highway Blockades
incidents.detail: Highway blockades at Santa Ana and Puente de Fierro.

incidents.title: Apatzingán Narcobloqueos
incidents.detail: Apatzingán-Buenavista federal highway blockaded with 18+ burning vehicles spanning 3.5 km. State police checkpoint overrun, 2 officers wounded. Road closed for 28 hours. Cartel members distributed flyers. Heavy military response deployed.

incidents.title: Uruapan Blockades and Fires
incidents.detail: Blockades and vehicle fires throughout Uruapan.

incidents.title: Sahuayo Coordinated Blockade
incidents.detail: Part of coordinated western Michoacán blockades.

incidents.title: Cancún Roadway Blockades
incidents.detail: Narcobloqueos at key intersections in Cancún area.

incidents.title: Playa del Carmen Blockades
incidents.detail: Narcobloqueos at main highway intersections.

incidents.title: Cozumel Roadway Blockades
incidents.detail: Narcobloqueos on island access routes.

incidents.title: Isla Mujeres Blockades
incidents.detail: Narcobloqueos on island access points.

incidents.title: Reynosa Massive Blockade Campaign
incidents.detail: Massive blockades with buses hijacked and burned. 36 narcobloqueos statewide. CDG-CJNG cooperation documented.

incidents.title: Nuevo Laredo Blockades and Fires
incidents.detail: Blockades and vehicle fires throughout Nuevo Laredo.

incidents.title: Miguel Alemán Border Fires
incidents.detail: Vehicle fires and blockades extended to border region.

incidents.detail: Narcobloqueos throughout Colima capital.

incidents.detail: Blockades on roads leading to Puerto Vallarta.

incidents.detail: Taxi burned on Avenida Internacional.

incidents.title: Tecate Highway Trailer Fire
incidents.detail: Tráiler burned on Autopista with gunfire on highway.

incidents.title: San Quintín Highway Vehicle Fires
incidents.detail: Vehicle burnings on highway.

incidents.title: Ensenada Fires and Arson
incidents.detail: Vehicle fires and business arsons. 29 total incidents across BC with zero deaths.

incidents.title: Puebla-Orizaba Highway Trailer Fire
incidents.detail: Trailer burned on autopista between Puebla and Orizaba.

incidents.title: Santa Rita Tlahuapan Truck Fire
incidents.detail: Truck burned on México-Puebla highway.

incidents.title: San Martín Texmelucan Toll Plaza Closure
incidents.detail: CAPUFE closed toll plazas at Esperanza and San Martín. Routes to Morelos blocked.

incidents.title: Mexico-Cuernavaca Highway Fire
incidents.detail: Vehicle fire at Tlalpan on Mexico-Cuernavaca highway at 5:30 PM. No injuries. Police and firefighters deployed. Central del Norte cancelled buses to Jalisco, Guanajuato, Michoacán, Aguascalientes.

incidents.detail: Narcobloqueos on federal highways.

incidents.title: Cuernavaca Blockades
incidents.detail: Narcobloqueos in Cuernavaca.

incidents.title: Puerto Vallarta Prison Break - 23 Inmates Escape
incidents.detail: Puerto Vallarta state penitentiary: 23 CJNG-affiliated inmates escaped during cartel diversionary operations. 1 prison custodian killed during breakout. 4 guards injured. Security perimeter breached on east fence. Escaped inmates joined cartel operations immediately.

incidents.title: El Tuli Killed in El Grullo - El Mencho's Right-Hand Man
incidents.detail: El Tuli (Luis Antonio López Cervantes), El Mencho's right-hand man and operations chief, executed in El Grullo by rival faction or military intelligence. 4 bodyguards killed in ambush on Calle Morelos. Vehicle destroyed. This killing destabilized CJNG command structure.

incidents.title: Atotonilco el Alto - National Guard Attack
incidents.detail: Atotonilco el Alto: CJNG gunmen attacked National Guard patrol on Carretera Atotonilco-Teocaltiche. 8 National Guard personnel killed in firefight, 3 wounded, 2 vehicles destroyed. Military reinforcements deployed. Major tactical victory for cartel operatives in region.

incidents.title: Autopista Guadalajara-Colima Tanker Fire at Cuatro Caminos
incidents.detail: Fuel tanker truck deliberately ignited on Autopista Guadalajara-Colima at Cuatro Caminos near Ciudad Guzmán. Massive explosion visible from 8 km away. Road completely impassable for 22 hours. 2 vehicles trapped, drivers evacuated. Environmental hazard reported.

incidents.title: La Piedad - Sicarios vs Police Confrontation
incidents.detail: La Piedad: Gunfight between Michoacán state police and armed sicarios on Avenida Independencia. 3 sicarios killed, 4 police officers wounded (1 critical). 2 vehicles seized. Cartel attempted to intimidate government forces.

incidents.title: Numarán - Trailer Burning on Carretera Zináparo-La Piedad
incidents.detail: Numarán: Cargo trailer burned on Carretera Zináparo-La Piedad Federal highway. 2 abandoned vehicles on roadway. Highway blockaded for 12 hours. Debris cleared by morning. Interstate commerce disrupted between Michoacán and Guanajuato.

incidents.title: Pénjamo - Passenger Bus Fire on Blvd Lázaro Cárdenas
incidents.detail: Pénjamo: Passenger bus (Autobuses Unidos route) deliberately burned on Boulevard Lázaro Cárdenas. 42 passengers evacuated safely before ignition. 3 vehicles nearby damaged by fire. CJNG leaflets distributed at scene. Road blocked for 4 hours.

incidents.title: Guanajuato Capital - 5 Additional Oxxo Stores Burned
incidents.detail: Guanajuato Capital: Coordinated burning of 5 Oxxo convenience stores on Calle Positos and Avenida Juárez. Stores looted before fires set. Cash registers emptied (estimated 850,000 pesos), merchandise destroyed. Fire spread to adjacent businesses. 2 fire trucks responded.

incidents.title: Tulum-Cobá Road - Multiple Vehicles Burned
incidents.detail: Highway 307 between Tulum and Cobá: 6 vehicles burned on roadside, 1 bus partially destroyed. Road closure affected tourist traffic to Cobá ruins. 1 civilian vehicle damaged with occupants escaping. Military checkpoint established by afternoon.

incidents.title: Cozumel - Bodega Aurrerá Fire
incidents.detail: Cozumel island: Bodega Aurrerá supermarket on Avenida 5 Norte deliberately torched. 2,000+ sq meters of retail space destroyed. Estimated loss of 12 million pesos. 3 adjacent businesses damaged. Fire contained by fire department after 2 hours.

incidents.title: Tepic-Xalisco Boulevard - Multiple Vehicles at Guayabo Curve
incidents.detail: Tepic-Xalisco boulevard at Guayabo curve: 4+ vehicles burned blocking road. Narco-message painted on nearby rock. Road impassable for 18 hours. Military presence increased in surrounding areas. Local residents reported heavy armed cartel activity.

incidents.title: México-Puebla Highway - Santa Rita Tlahuapan Incident (km 71-79)
incidents.detail: México-Puebla federal highway between km 71-79 near Santa Rita Tlahuapan: 3 vehicles burned, 1 truck overturned and torched. Road blockaded for 12 hours. Narcotics checkpoint suspected. Commercial traffic diverted to alternate routes.

incidents.title: Reynosa - Multiple Blockade Points
incidents.detail: Reynosa: Cartel established 5+ independent blockade points on Calle Hidalgo, Avenida Comercio, and Highway 2. 8 buses commandeered, 12 vehicles burned. Armed gunmen demanded 'cuota' (extortion tax) from commercial transporters. Blockades rotated with fresh personnel every 4 hours.

incidents.title: Jiutepec - Convenience Store Fire
incidents.detail: Jiutepec: Small convenience store (Soriana Express) on Avenida Las Flores deliberately burned. 3 adjacent buildings affected by fire spread. 1 person treated for smoke inhalation. Police presence minimal. Intimidation tactic by local criminal organization.

phase2.title: Autlán de Navarro Highway Blockades
phase2.detail: Multiple highway blockades on Federal Highway 80. Burning vehicles.

phase2.title: Lagos de Moreno Highway Vehicle Fire
phase2.detail: Vehicle set on fire on Lagos-Zapotlanejo highway near Jalostotitlán toll booth.

phase2.title: Military Operation Kills El Tuli
phase2.detail: Military operation kills El Tuli (Hugo César Macías Ureña), El Mencho's right-hand man who coordinated retaliation. Paracaidistas attempted arrest, suspect fled and opened fire. Seized 7.2M pesos and $965K USD.

phase2.title: Tonaya Confrontations
phase2.detail: Multiple confrontations with narcobloqueos and vehicles set on fire early Tuesday.

phase2.title: Cihuatlán Blockades and Fires
phase2.detail: Narcobloqueos with burning vehicles on Feb 24, confrontations continue Feb 25.

phase2.title: Tonalá Metro Confrontations
phase2.detail: Confrontations and vehicle fires Feb 23-25 in Tonalá metropolitan area.

phase2.title: Mazatlán Operativo Escudo Security Operations
phase2.detail: "Operativo Escudo" activated. GOES detained two armed men. Continued security operations.

shutdowns.title: Jalisco Statewide Shutdown
shutdowns.detail: 81 Oxxo stores damaged, 22 Banco del Bienestar branches affected. Chivas match cancelled. All schools suspended. Churches cancelled masses. 27 security personnel killed (25 National Guard, 1 prison guard, 1 FGE agent). Code Red activated.

shutdowns.title: Guanajuato Multi-Municipality Shutdown
shutdowns.detail: 70+ attacks in 23 of 46 municipalities. 200+ Oxxo stores damaged nationally. 18 Banco del Bienestar branches. Farmacias Guadalajara and transit buses burned.

shutdowns.title: Michoacán Regional Shutdown
shutdowns.detail: ~20 narcobloqueos across Tierra Caliente and western region. Apatzingán, Buenavista, Aguililla, Tepalcatepec, Zamora, Ecuandureo, Ixtlán, Tangancícuaro, Chilchota, La Piedad, Sahuayo, Pátzcuaro affected. C5 Michoacán activated emergency protocols.

disruptions.title: Lázaro Cárdenas Port Operations Suspended
disruptions.detail: Port operations temporarily suspended due to CJNG retaliation threats. Critical shipping delays for container cargo.

disruptions.title: Manzanillo Port Operations Disrupted
disruptions.detail: Port operations temporarily suspended. Resumed with Navy patrols, drone surveillance, Protection Level I.
disruptions.impact: Port operations resumed with enhanced security
